description:
Goal: make ordered function-head selection a first-class Compiler2 fact instead of clause-local rematching inside `LoweredBody`.

Change:
- Add `EntryDispatch(function)` or equivalent compiler-owned fact compiled from clause head patterns, parameter type preconditions, and clause guards.
- Consume dispatch-pure helper facts for guard calls and republish entry dispatch when clauses or helpers change.
- Publish clause/body outcomes in source order; single-clause functions still publish a trivial entry dispatch so consumers do not need a separate path.
- Feed the shared planner directly from Compiler2-owned inputs; do not synthesize a fake old-lowerer intermediate just to get through planning.
- Make clause selection authoritative for function entry so body-local clause-head rematching becomes deletion territory, not a parallel implementation to preserve.

Why:
- Compiler2 should not keep reimplementing clause dispatch in its body lowerer.
- One entry-dispatch fact gives the same model for user code, runtime code, and later body-site dispatch planning.

Checks:
- Multi-clause functions with preconditions and helper guards publish stable entry-dispatch facts.
- Shared-prefix factoring and clause reachability follow `dispatch_matrix` semantics.
- Redefining a helper or clause updates only dependent entry-dispatch facts.

Non-goals:
- No body-site dispatch planning yet.
- No semantic call/type/executable facts yet; that remains `fz-rh2.6.2`.
- No transitional path where Compiler2 keeps both authoritative entry dispatch and authoritative body-local clause dispatch.
title: Compiler2 dispatch: derive function entry plans from clause heads
